title: Boat Rental in Cesme: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Cesme, on Turkey's Aegean coast, offers boat hire for as little as 150 EUR for a half-day small motorboat. Depart from Cesme Marina to explore nearby bays and islands. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can compare verified boat listings and book your trip.

Where to set off in Cesme

Cesme's main departure point is Cesme Marina, a modern facility with full services. Nearby, Dalyan Marina in Dalyan village offers a quieter alternative. For short trips, the small harbour at Ilica is ideal for accessing Ilica Plaji and the surrounding coves. Further afield, the port of Alacati, just 8 km away, provides access to the popular bays of Sifne and Kizilkum. The Greek island of Chios is less than 10 nautical miles from Cesme, making it a popular day trip destination.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cesme?

Prices range from 150 EUR for a half-day small motorboat to 1,500 EUR for a full-day crewed yacht. For a budget-friendly option, consider a shared boat tour at around 50 EUR per person. A half-day rental of a 7-metre motorboat without skipper starts from 150 EUR, while a full-day catamaran for up to 8 guests costs approximately 800 EUR. A crewed luxury motor yacht, such as the Azimut 58, can cost from 1,200 EUR for a day. Compare prices and availability through day charters in Cesme on BoatHire24.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (up to 6 guests), ideal for couples or small groups exploring nearby coves; from 150 EUR for half a day.
  2. Sailing yacht (up to 8 guests), perfect for a relaxed day on the water with wind power; from 400 EUR for a full day.
  3. Catamaran (up to 12 guests), spacious and stable, great for families or groups; from 800 EUR for a full day.
  4. Crewed luxury motor yacht (up to 10 guests), includes skipper and crew for a VIP experience; from 1,200 EUR for a full day. For example, the Azimut 58 can be booked via the Azimut 58 charter page.
  5. RIB or speedboat (up to 8 guests), fast and fun for watersports and sightseeing; from 250 EUR for half a day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cesme?

For most small motorboats under 15 hp, no licence is required in Turkey, but larger boats may require a valid ICC or equivalent. Always confirm with the operator: they will specify exact requirements based on the boat's size and engine power. For crewed charters, a licence is not needed as the skipper holds the necessary qualifications.

Try to book a morning departure to avoid the afternoon wind, known locally as the meltemi, which picks up around 2 PM and can make the sea choppy for small boats.

At a glance

  • Price band: 150, 1,500 EUR per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: half day (4 hours) to full day (8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 2 to 12 passengers
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Cesme

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Cesme?

Yes, for smaller boats under 15 hp you can rent bareboat without a skipper, provided you have no licence required or a valid ICC.

What is the best time of year for boat rental in Cesme?

The best time is between May and October, with calm seas and warm weather; July and August are the peak months for high season.

How far is it from Cesme to Chios by boat?

The crossing to Chios harbour is approximately 8 nautical miles and takes about 45 minutes by fast motorboat.

Are there any extra costs like fuel or mooring fees?

Fuel is usually included in the rental price for half- and full-day charters, but mooring fees at stops like Dalyan Marina may be extra; check with the operator.
